title: bufonia macrocarpa var. elata
intro: Bufonia macrocarpa var. elata, also known as the 'Gibraltar rock-rose', is a plant species in the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, pink flowers and woody stems. The medicinal properties of Bufonia macrocarpa var. elata include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of plumbagin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of using this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are applied topically to treat various health conditions.
